summary:
Hertie was a chain of department stores that was taken over by Karstadt in 1994. Founded in 1882 under the name Tietz (Hertie is the abbreviation for Hermann Tietz), it was „Aryanised“ in 1933.

Links

  • Wikipedia on Hertie (German)
title: Hertie

summary:
Karstadt is a department store chain founded in 1881. Renamed ‘Galeria’ in 2020. ‘Aryanised’ in 1933.

Links:

  • Wikipedia on Karstadt
title: Karstadt

summary:
Kaufhof was a department store chain that merged with Metro in 1996 and with Karstadt in 2020. Founded in 1879 under the name Tietz, it was ‘Aryanised’ in 1933.

Weitere Quellen

  • Wikipedia Galeria Kaufhof
title: Kaufhof

summary: „Union“ is a trade name of the company Sinram & Wendt (in German) based in Hamelin. The company was deleted from the company register in 2013.
title: Union

summary: „Kleiderbügel“ (Clothes Hangers) started as a spin-off of Blaufußtölpel: At some point, even the hottest fashion trends are ready for the closet, having exceeded their half-life or simply fallen out of favor. That’s when clothes hangers come into their own. Some of these practical helpers from days gone by are documented here. Many of the examples shown here also offer insight into local economic history and the past diversity of retail as advertising media.
title: Spin-off "Kleiderbügel" launched

summary: After this blog lay dormant for about two years, it was time for some “spring cleaning.” New posts will now appear weekly (until mid-2026, according to current estimates). In addition, there is now a map view that visualizes the origin of the hangers (where possible).
title: Improvements 2024

summary:
This page has been slightly revised for an upcoming exhibition:

  • Navigation has been slightly improved
  • The current coat hanger is now displayed on the home page
  • The map is larger and has greater contrast
  • There is now a search function
  • Loading time has been significantly reduced
  • Statistics on the coat hangers are now available
  • An English translation is available
  • jQuery has been removed; the clothes rail visualization is now implemented with Swiper JS

More information about the exhibition will be available in 2026!
title: Improvements 2025
